content: history.pushState()/replaceState() do NOT themselves fire a popstate event -- popstate only fires on actual back/forward browser action (or history.back()/forward()/go()). popstate carries no explicit direction (back vs forward); direction must be inferred via sequence numbers/timestamps in the state object. Browsers (WebKit specifically) also gate popstate/pushState behavior on user-interaction provenance for security, and behavior is not fully cross-browser consistent.
